MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home
  • Development Library
  • Examples
  • Release Notes
MWCCPlayer_DRLib C++

Magewell SDK supports capturing, decoding, rendering and recording the CEA-708 and CEA-608 captions in Windows x86 and x64 with Magewell Pro Capture family cards. Functions related to CC signals are also provided, including setting whether to display captions, parsing captions in EIA-608 or CEA-708 standard, and customizing fonts, foreground and background colors, and resolution of CC captions. The example is compatible with SDI signals with or without CC data. Compared with MWCCPlayer_DShow C++, this example works more efficiently because of the dedicated interfaces.

Folder dir: SDKv3\Examples\VC++\GUI\MWCCPlayer_DRLib

Supported Hardware

Pro Capture Family and Eco capture Family cards which support SDI signals

Features

  1. Preview captured videos (without audio data) and record them into MP4 files.
  2. Capture, decode, render and record CC data into MP4 files.
  3. Customize font, resolution, foreground color and background color of captions.

Workflow

  1. To initialize a CC decoder and a renderer, call: MWCreateCC708Decoder, MWCreateRender, MWSetCC708DecoderCallback, MWSetCC708DecodeType, MWEnableOutputChannel, MWLoadFont
  2. To initialize and enumerate all available devices, call: MWCaptureInitInstance, MWRefreshDevice, MWGetChannelCount, MWGetChannelInfoByIndex, MWGetDevicePath,
  3. To open the selected channel and start to capture video and closed captions, call: MWOpenChannelByPath, MWCreateVideoCapture, MWCaptureSetSDIANCType, MWRegisterNotify : MWCAP_NOTIFY_NEW_SDI_ANC_PACKET , MWCaptureGetSDIANCPacket
  4. To render video and CC using callback function, call: MWDecodeCC708, MWRenderCC608BufferArea, MWRenderCC708BufferArea
  5. To specify decode properties, call: MWSetCC708DecodeType, MWEnableOutputChannel, MWDisableOutputChannel
  6. To specify render properties, call: MWLoadFont
  7. To stop capturing and rendering, call: MWDestoryVideoCapture, MWUnregisterNotify, MWCloseChannel
  8. To create MP4 videos with closed captions, follow the steps below.
    1. To initialize an MP4 file, call: mw_mp4_open
    2. To write video and subtitle tracks, call: mw_mp4_set_video, mw_mp4_set_subtitle
      To write video and subtitle frames, call: mw_mp4_write_video, mw_mp4_write_subtitle
    3. To close an MP4 file, call: mw_mp4_close

Flow Chart

The main thread of MWCCPlayer_DRLib
CC Capture thread of MWCCPlayer_DRLib
MP4 Save

Result

  1. Run the MWCCPlayer_DRLib.exe.
  2. Select the capture device from the Devices menu.
    Select capture device
  3. Check Get Closed Caption to enable the CC feature.
    Get Closed Caption

  4. Specify CC settings.

    The Freetype 2.8 library is used for character rendering. The default font is SourceCodePro-Regular, which is an open source font provided by the Adobe company which can be used free of charge either by business or individuals.

    CC Settings
